# Components of Computers


Computers are made up of **hardware** and **software**.



## Hardware


The **physical** **components** of a computer system that can be **touched** and **seen**.



### Motherboard


1. A **circuit** **board** that **allows** **data** to **travel** to the **different** **components** in a computer.
2. The **motherboard** is the **main** **circuit** **board** that **connects** all the **components** of a computer.



### Central Processing Unit (CPU)


1. The **component **of a computer that **carries** **out** and **controls** the computer's **instructions**.
2. The **central** **processing** **unit** **(CPU**) is the **brain** of the computer. It **executes** **instructions** and **performs** **calculations**.
3. It is split into three main subcomponents:
  1. Control unit (CU) - controls the **order **and **processing **of **processes**.
  2. Arithmetic-logic unit (ALU) - computer **arithmetic **and **logic operations**.
  3. Registers - temporarily **store **small amounts of **data **needed by the CPU.



### Primary Memory


The **component** of a computer that **stores** **data** and **instructions **that the computer needs to run and execute instructions.

  1. **RAM (Random Access Memory)**: **Temporary** **storage** for **data** and **instructions** that are **currently** **being** **used** by the CPU.
  2. **ROM (Read-Only Memory)**: **Permanent** **storage** for **essential** **instructions** that **cannot** be **modified**. Stores the necessary data that the computer **needs **to **boot**.
  3. **Cache**: **Small **but **extremely fast** memory that **stores frequently used data** for **fast retrieval**.

<callout type="note">Upgrading the RAM to be faster and/or feature a higher capacity results in significant improvement of the speed and amount of available running processes on a computer.</callout>



### Storage


The **component** of a computer that **stores** **data** and **instructions** **permanently. **Usually used for storing user data.

There are two types of storage:

  1. **Hard Disk Drive (HDD)**: **Magnetic** **storage** that **uses** **spinning** **disks** to **store** **data**. Weaker than SSD, but much cheaper and commonly has more capacity.
  2. **Solid State Drive (SSD)**: **Flash** **storage** that **uses** **memory** **chips** to **store** **data**. Much more powerful and faster, but more expensive.



### Graphics and Sound Components


The **components** of a computer that **process** **and** **output** **visual** and **audio** **data**.

  1. **Graphics Processing Unit (GPU)**: **Processes** **visual** **data** and **outputs** it to a **display** **device**.
  2. **Sound Card**: **Processes** **audio** **data** and **outputs** it to **speakers** or **headphones**.

<callout type="note">GPUs are actually very powerful **processors **(similarly to CPUs) that compute many **complex calculations at the same time** to allow for powerful graphic features.</callout>



### Power Supply


The **component** of a computer that **converts** **AC** **power** from an **outlet** into **DC** **power** for the **computer**.

The **power** **supply** **converts** **AC** **power** from an **outlet** into **DC** **power** for the **computer**.



### Input and Output Devices


<definition term="Input Device">A **device** that **sends** **data** and **instructions** to a computer.</definition>

<definition term="Output Devices">A **device** that **receives** **data** and **instructions** from a computer and **displays** **them** to the **user**.</definition>

1. **Input devices**: **Send** **data** and **instructions** to a computer (e.g., **keyboard**, **mouse**, **microphone**).
2. **Output devices**: **Receive** **data** and **instructions** from a computer and **display** **them** to the **user** (e.g., **monitor**, **printer**, **speakers**).



### User Interfaces


The **means** by which **human** **users** **interact** with a **digital** **technology**. The **intent** is to make the **user's** **experience** **straight** **forward**, **intuitive**, and **requiring** **minimum** **effort** to **achieve** the **desired** **outcome**.

1. **Graphical User Interface (GUI)**: **Uses** **visual** **elements** like **icons** and **windows** to **interact** with the **user**.
2. **Command Line Interface (CLI)**: **Uses** **text** **commands** to **interact** with the **user**.



### Haptic Interfaces


1. A **user** **interface** that **provides** **feedback** to the **user** through **touch**.
2. **Haptic** **interfaces** **provide** **feedback** to the **user** through **touch** (e.g., **vibrations** in a **game** **controller**).

<callout type="example">Sony PlayStation's DualSense game controllers use haptic feedback on some of their buttons to make gaming more immersive and difficult in certain scenarios.</callout>



## Software


The **programs** and **applications** that **run** on a **computer**.



### Operating Systems


1. Software that **manages** the **hardware**, **software** and **memory** of a computer as well as **providing** a **user** **interface**.
2. The **operating** **system** is the **software** that **manages** the **hardware**, **software**, and **memory** of a computer. It **provides** a **user** **interface** for **interacting** with the **system**.

<callout type="example">* Some of the most common operating systems used on personal computers include **Windows**, **macOS**, and **Linux**.
* Mobile phones commonly run **Android **or **iOS **as their operating system.</callout>



### Applications / Applicative Software


1. Software that **serves** a **specific** **purpose**, such as a **word** **processor** or **video** **editor**.
2. **Software** **applications** are **programs** that **perform** **specific** **tasks** (e.g., **word** **processors**, **web** **browsers**, **games**).



### Malicious Software (Malware)


1. Software **designed** to **steal** **data** or **damage** **computers**/ **IT** **systems**.
2. Common **types **of malware include **ransomware**, **viruses**, **trojan horses**, etc.



<callout type="note">Computer systems are made up of **hardware **and **software**. **Hardware includes **the **physical components **of a computer, while **software **includes the **programs **and **applications **that **run **on the computer system.</callout>



<callout type="self_review">1. What are the main components of a computer? 
2. What is the difference between hardware and software? 
3. What is the role of the operating system? 
4. What is the difference between input and output devices? 
5. What is malware and why does it pose an issue to digital security?</callout>



<callout type="tok">How do the components of a computer work together to perform tasks? What are the ethical implications of using computers in everyday life?</callout>
